observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation from the prior year’s audit that the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records and
coordinate with the Registry of Deeds for the titled lots in the name of the
Municipality.
We further recommended that registration and transfer of title of the land
owned by the Municipality be prioritized to fully protect its interest.

We recommended that Management require the a) Inventory Committee to
properly fill up the RPCPPE; b) Municipal Treasurer to have the AREs/PAR
renewed/updated; and c) Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal
Treasurer to trace the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that Management require the Assistant Municipal
Treasurer/OIC to (a) secure all stale checks and the supporting documents, inform
the payees thereof, cancel the same and furnish the Accounting Office with the
original DVs for the preparation of JEV debiting the Cash in Bank, and crediting
Accounts Payable and Prior Years’ Adjustment for stale checks less than two years
and for those more than two years, respectively; and (b) henceforth, regularly
monitor all outstanding checks so that written notices can be sent to the payees a
month before these become stale and, together with the Municipal Accountant,
determine stale checks requiring issuance of replacement checks. Likewise, the
Municipal Accountant should be able to trace the source of the bank credit of
₱100,000 to prepare the necessary adjustment for the book reconciling item
disclosed as an unrecorded deposit.

The Municipal Accountant, during the exit conference, explained that the source
of the bank credit had not yet been traced. The Municipal Engineer, who is the
coordinator of the Sagana Project to which the funds were downloaded, commented that
no additional funding for the aforementioned project was obtained. On the other hand, the
Municipal Treasurer had committed to communicate with the Bureau of Treasury to trace
the source of the bank credit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) conduct a bidding for medicines based on estimated quantities and kind
of drugs anticipated to be prescribed for a period of three months or
less at the option of management, which shall serve as basis for the
choice of pharmacy with whom the Municipality shall enter into an
agreement for the purchase of medicines under the program;
b) include in the Memorandum of Agreement with the chosen pharmacy
the provision that only claims supported with complete documents are
honored by the pharmacy and to see to it that the claim stub provided
by the MSWD is signed by the recipient; and
c) require the Municipal Social Welfare Office to prepare individual
checklists for each type of assistance, to attach a checklist to the
Certificate of Eligibility, and to see to it that the documents required
under DSWD AO No. 1, s. of 2011 are available and attached thereto
before releasing the Certificate of Eligibility to the client.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a) require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the corresponding expense
account the subsequent issuance/distribution of the welfare goods duly
supported by the RIS and RSMI, record in the books the inventory of
welfare goods for distribution which are still in the custody of the
Municipality based in the inventory report prepared and Prepare and
maintain Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each inventory of welfare/ relief goods
procured by the Municipality; and
b) require the Supply/ Property Officer to coordinate with the MDRRM Officer
and MSWD Officer in preparing and submitting, in the prescribed format,
an Inventory List/Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution in the custody of
the Municipality as of December 31, 2021, prepare and maintain Stock
Cards, Requisition and Issue Slip, and Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued for welfare goods and conduct the periodic physical count of
inventory and prepare the corresponding RPCI.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal
Treasurer to cause the inclusion of the warranty clause in the succeeding contracts/
POs under the Emergency Procurement of the Bayanihan Act, to ensure assurance
of replacement of/ recovery on the goods delivered with defects and/or which do not
conform to the quality standards and technical specifications of the contract, and
ensure that all Contracts/POs contain detailed item specifications pursuant to COA
Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996, to protect the interest of the
Government.

We recommended and Management agreed that in all procurement-related
activities of the Municipality, strict compliance with the provisions of RA 9184 and
the revised IRR must be observed. In this regard, the BAC should prepare and
review the checklist for all biddings conducted and ensure that all the procedures
required therein are complied with or observed to preserve the integrity of the
bidding process.

We also recommended that personnel in charge of accepting and inspection
of deliveries indicate in the Inspection and Acceptance Report that date of receipt
and conduct of inspection.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to proper and
correct classification of accounts. Consequently, require reclassification
of the inventory items to the appropriate inventory accounts. A supplies
ledger card for each type of inventory shall also be maintained;
b) for monitoring, control and accountability, an Inventory Custodian Slip
(ICS) shall be prepared upon issuance of small tangible items with life of
more than one year covered by the approved Requisition and Issue Slip
(RIS); and
c) personnel in charge of receiving and inspecting procured items should
completely fill up the Inspection and Acceptance Report especially the
date of receipt and inspection.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) Require the Provincial Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments for
the reconciling items requiring addition and deduction in the
Province’s books of accounts;
(b) Require the Provincial Treasurer to secure all stale checks and the
supporting documents, cancel the same and furnish the Accounting
Office with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DVs), for the
preparation of JEV debiting the Cash in Bank, and crediting
Accounts Payable and Prior Years’ Adjustment for stale checks less
than two (2) years and for those more than two (2) years, respectively,
and henceforth regularly monitor all outstanding checks so that
written notices can be sent to the payees a month before these become
stale; and
(c) Require the Provincial Treasurer and the Provincial Accountant to
regularly monitor and determine stale checks requiring the issuance
of replacement checks.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor:
(a) require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash
advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997;
(b) direct the Provincial Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation
of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it that no additional
cash advance are granted unless the previous ones are settled or properly
accounted for; and
(c) instruct the Provincial Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/validation of all
outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers and employees with long
outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions when necessary, such as
withholding of their salaries as provided in the Circular and, if warranted,
request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the
procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Provincial Governor require (a) the Inventory
Committee to conduct an actual count of inventories and prepare the RPCI; and (b)
the GSO and the Provincial Accountant to maintain property records and reconcile
the same to assure the existence and reliability of the Inventory accounts.

We further recommended that the Provincial Accountant and GSO trace
the whereabouts of the recorded inventories which may have already been
issued and consumed and make the necessary adjustment in order to present
fairly the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Provincial Accountant
and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain their respective subsidiary
records to facilitate reconciliation with the general ledger controlling account
pursuant to Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management enjoin the concerned department
heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the
local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective
duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
October 23, 2015.

We recommended that, henceforth, the GSO and the inventory team
prepare the RPCPPE by type of PPE required in Section 124 and Annex 49 of
the NGAs Manual for LGUs, reconcile the results of the physical count with
the corresponding PPE accounts in the books, investigate variances and charge
the value of unaccounted properties to personnel accountable thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Provincial
Accountant to review the recorded payables which have remained outstanding
for more than two years to ascertain if these still represent valid claims,
otherwise, seek authority from the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to revert these
to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund so that these may be made
available to finance other developmental projects and activities.

We recommended that the concerned Heads of Offices submit
supporting documents to substantiate the payment of AHDP, such as:
a. Authority from the Agency Head/Office that the frontline HRDs
physically report to assigned work station on the specified official
working hours;
b. Report as to the number of COVID-19 patients attended/monitored;
c. Daily status/monitoring report on the COVID-19 patients
quarantined in the assigned facilities; and
d. Actual work done in the assigned quarantine facility.

We recommended that Management require the concerned office
responsible in the distribution of welfare goods and medical supplies to submit
copies of distribution lists that contain the name and signature of recipients as
well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the
kind and quantity of items received to facilitate accounting, thereof.

We further recommended that the approving officer/Head of the PE
ensure that POs are completely filled out with detailed specifications and other
relevant information to allow comparison with the prevailing market prices in
the locality, or with the existing price data of the agency, the Department of
Trade and Industry or other relevant agencies.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to comply with Section 3.5
GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020, on the inclusion of a warranty
clause in the Purchase Order to protect the government’s interest against
manufacturing defects of delivered goods.

We recommended and the Provincial Engineer’s Office agreed to
ensure that signboards contain the relevant and updated information on the
essential features/elements of the infrastructure projects being implemented to
promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the PDRRMO coordinate with the Office of
Civil Defense (OCD) in the preparation and submission of the Province’s
PSCP pursuant to NDRRMC Memorandum No 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the
following activities:
a. To conduct risk assessment;
b. To identify critical processes and functions;
c. To determine scenarios that made disrupt normal operation;
d. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical
processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to
eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined
disruptions following the prescribed templates and requirements
in the PSCP guidebook; and
f. To submit plan to the PDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief
Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the
passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents
required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the Audit Team to
properly evaluate the propriety of the land purchase.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the
provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-DND Joint Circular No.2015-01 dated January
8, 2015, in granting the cash advance for CF by ensuring that cash advances are
supported by documents required in the circular. Further, to support the release
and use of the Confidential Fund, Management should see to it that the peace and
order situation of the Province requires confidential activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) coordinate with the Punong Barangays for the submission of beneficiary/household profiles and require written authorization from recipients other than the identified target beneficiaries. We further recommended that the MDRRMO submit an explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: The required documents had been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop recording purchases of non-expendable supplies directly to the related expense account and record the purchases and utilization of these items in accordance with Sections 113 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to fairly present the asset and expense accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The adjusting entry to take up the non-expendable supplies had been recorded, the succeeding procurements of the same nature had been recorded to the appropriate asset account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a special account in the General Fund to record the receipt and utilization of the Bayanihan grant pursuant to LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 to facilitate monitoring of the receipt and utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: A special account had been set up to record the Bayanihan grant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize the LDRRMF in accordance with Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013. We further recommended that Management stop charging honorarium/incentive against the LDRMMF.
observations.managementAction: The payments for honorarium and incentives had been stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of procurement through reimbursement and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and relevant GPPB issuances to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process. We also recommended that Management comply with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant and utilization of cash advances. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before processing any claims.
observations.managementAction: The procurement of goods through reimbursement had been stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop transferring unexpended LDRRMF from the STF to GF before the lapse of five years and to require the Municipal Accountant to record charges in the STF in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management had stopped the transferring of unexpended LDRRMF from STF to GF before the lapse of the five-year regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The copies of the required reports had been submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the PE to properly and completely fill up the relevant and required information in the POs to allow comparison with the prevailing market prices in the locality, existing price data of the agency, the Department of Trade and Industry or other relevant agencies
observations.managementAction: The POs had been completely filled out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of transferring savings from the completed projects under the 20% DF to the Trust Fund for the payment of honorarium to the municipal officials and employees involved in government procurement. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that sources of funds for the payment of honoraria are in accordance with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007. In case of deficiency in the collections from procurement activities and non-availability of savings, the amount of honoraria shall be adjusted proportionately for all those entitled thereto in accordance with Section 3.5 of the Budget Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management had stopped using the savings from 20% DF for the payment of honoraria.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management agreed to submit justification/s for the noted deficiencies. We further recommend that, henceforth, Management adhere to the provisions of DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2 dated September 23, 2016 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 97-273 dated October 30, 1997 in the conduct of lakbay-aral or benchmarking activity.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted justifications and the other pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from holding seminars/ workshops outside of the province in accordance with pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012to avoid extravagant expenditures and wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management had refrained conducting seminars/workshops outside the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure compliance with Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the required warranty security, otherwise, they will be held answerable for negligence in case of structural defects or failures within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond.
observations.managementAction: The warranty security posted had already complied with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare one payroll for each office for the monthly salaries of officials and employees, with all the allowable deductions, and prepare two separate cash advance vouchers equivalent to the net amount of the payroll covering the pay period for the 15th and end of the month.
observations.managementAction: A payroll had been made per office and complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the documentary requirements, such as the (a) number of personnel involved and their corresponding rates/salary; (b) schedule of work and places of assignments or station; (c) group classification of personnel to determine the Equivalent Equipment Monthly Statutory Minimum Wage Rate in accordance with the applicable Rule Implementing RA No. 6727; and (c) approved documents indicating the minimum requirements of the agency on the number of security personnel to be involved in the project. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant review and ensure the completeness of documentation in the processing of payments for services in accordance with Item 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 specifically in the appropriation of funds to support implementation of MCPC prioritized plans/programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management had incorporated in its Annual Budget the mandated appropriation for MCPC.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer Designee to issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to concerned municipal officials and employees to pinpoint responsibility and accountability for municipal properties.
observations.managementAction: The PARs/AREs had been updated and AAR submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
• Required the personnel in charge in the management of fund to intensify efforts to locate the beneficiary/ borrower. Send demand letters in a periodic basis before employing legal means to collect the receivables.
• Conduct proper screening of would be beneficiaries. Conduct periodic monitoring of the borrower’s performance and steps to assist the beneficiaries in problems encountered has to be handy and available.
• Make available a continuing training on capability building and technical assistance for the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The collection letters had been sent to the delinquent borrowers been rolled out to the subsequent/targeted new beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation for the apparent disregard of the basic requirements on pakyaw labor contracting, particularly on clearly defined schedule of work, procurement of labor component of the project only, provision of tools to the pakyaw group, and notarized pakyaw agreement. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it those succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The pertinent documents had been duly submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all movable properties of the Municipality; (b) the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO to prepare the report on the results of the count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results with the accounting records; and (c) the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO as property custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and ledgers cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The inventory report had already been reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.reason: The inventory report was not duly filled out, and AREs had not been updated as reiterated in the part of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board refer the questioned expenditure for resolution to the Dep-Ed Central Office for final resolution by the Dep-ED, DBM, DILG. Henceforth, ensure that only those priority expense items as enumerated under Section 4.0 of DEPED/DBM/DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2017 are included in the SEF budget approved and enacted.
observations.managementAction: The expenditures of the same nature were no longer charged to SEF.
observations.reason: No action was initiated by the Local School Board on this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review and identify the individual accounts composing the bail bonds payable. Those that were able to fulfill the purpose of the bond will be refunded. Identify also the individual accounts of the Customers Deposit Payable and initiate refund or forfeited if the purpose of the deposit was already complied. For those who failed to comply with the purpose of the bail bond/customers deposit, such shall be forfeited in favor of the municipality. Prepare the necessary journal entries to record the refund and/or forfeiture of the payable and furnish COA with the JEV.
observations.managementAction: Some bail bonds had been refunded.
observations.reason: The documents relative to these bonds required and presented in the court hence, there is a possibility that refunds will be made only after the case is resolved. The Customers Deposit Payable pertained to deposits for space rental in the new public market, which can only be returned or forfeited after the tenants’ non-renewal of the contract.

observations.recommendation:
• Required the concerned personnel to submit the requirements of transactions suspended in audit to facilitate settlement.
• Required the Municipal Treasurer to enforce the immediate collection of disallowances to those of the deceased/separated employees.
observations.managementAction: The disallowances partly settled.
observations.reason: There had been no Request for Partial Settlement of ND, duly approved by COA hence, no adjustments could be made on the books. The unsettled disallowances pertain to those of the deceased/separated employees.

observations.recommendation: Directed the GAD Focal Person to initiate activities for the establishment of a local GAD code based on the gender issues and concerns in their respective localities identified through consultation with women constituents. Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) in the formulation and development of the said Code. Make representation with the local law-making body for the eventual passage and approval of the Local GAD code.
observations.managementAction: The management had commenced the formulation of the GAD Code.
observations.reason: The GAD Code has not been fully established.

observations.recommendation: Cause the immediate refund of the amount of ₱290,000.00 paid to all appointed officials and employees of the municipality considering that the disbursement of funds was devoid of any legal basis.
observations.managementAction: The grant of the allowance had been stopped and partial settlement had been made.
observations.reason: There had been no Request for Partial Settlement of the ND, duly approved by COA hence, no adjustments could be effected. The unsettled disallowances are those of the deceased/separated employees.

observations.recommendation: Required the immediate refund of the overpayment as shown on the attached Notice of Disallowance No. 2011-001-100(10) dated March 21, 2011 is hereby recommended. Henceforth, stop the practice of paying benefits more than what is authorized by laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The disallowance had already been recorded in the books and repayment was already started.
observations.reason: There had been no Request for Partial Settlement of the ND, duly approved by COA hence, no adjustments could be effected. The unsettled disallowances are those of the deceased/separated employees.

observations.recommendation: Directed all persons held liable therefore to immediately settle in full the disallowed amount so received otherwise, legal measures shall be resorted to if only to collect the same.
observations.managementAction: The repayments had been made in installments through monthly payroll deductions.
observations.reason: There had been no Request for Partial Settlement of the ND, duly approved by COA hence, no adjustments could be effected. The unsettled disallowances are those of the deceased/separated employees.

observations.recommendation: Directed each recipient of the excessive amount of PEI to refund the sum not legally due them or the aggregate amount of P1,495,000.00. Henceforth, observe strictly the guidelines prescribed in the grant of allowances and other benefits to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The disallowance had already been recorded in the books and repayment was already started.
observations.reason: There had been no Request for Partial Settlement of the ND, duly approved by COA hence, no adjustments could be effected. The unsettled disallowances are those of the deceased/separated employees.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the properties in the name of the municipality by coordinating with the Municipal Assessor, DENR and other related agencies for the processing of the needed documents.
observations.managementAction: Management had been waiting for the free titling from the DENR.
observations.reason: This had been reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop charging the Construction of the Multi-Purpose Park to the 20% DF and reimburse that fund from the General Fund Proper for the amount already disbursed. We further recommended that, henceforth, the PDC strictly observe DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the identification of prioritized projects chargeable against the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The PPDO strictly checked the projects charged to 20% Development Fund to ensure these comply with the provisions of The Updated Guidelines on the Appropriation and Utilization of the 20% DF under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Accountant strictly observe the guidelines prescribed in the recording of the receipt and utilization of subsequent fund releases from the national government to facilitate monitoring and tracking of the grant’s utilization.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2020 The grant was recorded to General Fund Proper under account Due to NGAs – Bayanihan Grant for easy monitoring and tracking of the fund utilization and to facilitate the preparation of the required liquidation report.

The prescribed guidelines on the recording of receipt and utilization had been strictly observed in the subsequent fund releases.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Engineer determine the proper corrections to be done and thereafter, the Provincial Governor require the contractors concerned to effect the necessary corrections as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration.
Henceforth, require the assigned Project Engineers to continuously supervise and monitor during the implementation of projects to ensure good quality workmanship.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019 The defects of the projects had already been corrected.
The PEO regularly supervised and monitored the implementations of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the BAC to strictly observe the allowable period in the award and implementation of infrastructure projects as prescribed under the Revised IRR of RA 9184, in order to establish the propriety/regularity of the procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 The BAC had strictly observed the allowable period in the award and implementation of infrastructure projects. Further, Notice of Awards had been posted in the PHILGEP.

observations.recommendation: Direct the GSO to renew vehicle registration with the LTO.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 The GSO had already updated the registration of the motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Provincial Governor require (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct an actual count of inventories and prepare the RPCI; (b) the GSO and the Provincial Accountant to maintain property records and reconcile both records; (c) the requisitioning office to prepare and submit the RIS to the GSO on a weekly basis; and (d) the GSO to consolidate on the SSMI all RIS and submit the same to the Accountant weekly, as basis for recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The GSO had assigned personnel to record the current purchases in the stock cards. RISs were prepared for the issuance of supplies distributed to the end-users. SSMI was maintained in hard and soft copies for ready review. Restrictions imposed during the COVID-19 pandemic prevented the Inventory Committee from conducting the physical count. Lastly, there was a lack of personnel in the Accounting Office to maintain the individual SLC of each inventory item.
observations.reason: The absence of available records of inventories acquired in the prior years made it difficult to prepare the breakdown of inventories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Accountant (a) make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of all accounts affected; (b) verify the status of funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies; (c) exert effort to locate records pertaining to the unidentified accounts; and (d) strengthen the review over the work of subordinate personnel to avoid errors in recording to ensure the fair presentation of financial statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The Accounting Office had already adjusted the identified income of ₱279,809.57. The Management was still locating the MOA and other documents supporting the remaining balance in the account Due to LGU as the basis for adjustment. Personnel were closely supervised to ensure accurate recording of transactions.
observations.reason: It was difficult to locate the corresponding MOA and other documents because the personnel-in-charge was already separated from the service, hence, could not identify the nature of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Provincial Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain their respective subsidiary records to facilitate reconciliation with the general ledger controlling account and assigned personnel to coordinate with the Register of Deeds as regards the titling of the remaining lots owned by the Province.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The GSO had prepared a database for the Provincial Government’s real properties based on the records of the Provincial Assessor. The office had initiated data gathering to offices and stakeholders.
observations.reason: The GSO and Accounting Office lacked the personnel to maintain an updated property record and conduct reconciliation. There were no funds allocated for processing documents for the transfer of ownership to the Provincial Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO and the Provincial Accountant reconcile the physical count of movable properties and the balances per books in order that the accounts will be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The GSO had already furnished the accounting with RPCPPE to facilitate the reconciliation of their records. There was a difficulty in verifying properties acquired in the previous years due to inadequate property records.
observations.reason: No personnel was assigned to focus on the reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Accountant thoroughly review and analyze the recorded A/Ps in order to come up with a final list of obligations with no valid claimants and seek authority from the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to revert these to the unappropriated surplus so that these may be used for other beneficial purposes.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The Sangguniang Panlalawigan had passed a resolution reverting the identified obligations with no valid claimant.
observations.reason: There were recorded obligations that could not be identified due to the unavailability of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Provincial Health Officer, Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and other concerned personnel to submit copies of distribution lists duly acknowledged by the recipients to validate actual distribution. We further recommended that the distribution lists should contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to facilitate accounting for the medical supplies and to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 The Provincial Health Officer had submitted a partial list on the distribution of rapid tests and other medical supplies.
observations.reason: The utilization of rapid test was supported only with the names of the recipients but without signatures to avoid and minimize the spread of the virus.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the PE to properly and completely fill up the relevant and required information in the POs to allow comparison with the prevailing market prices in the locality, or with the existing price data of the agency, the Department of Trade and Industry or other relevant agencies.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The requisitioning offices were advised to indicate the detailed specifications of the items requested and the GSO to reflect the complete specifications, including the brand names of the items in the PO.
observations.reason: The item specifications were indicated in the Purchase Requests, however, the specific brand name of said items were not reflected in the PO and the Inspection Acceptance Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit the basis for setting the ABC of the thermal handheld scanner at P65,000.00 per unit as well as an explanation on the material price difference. We further recommended that, henceforth, for the Province’s procurement activities, the BAC conduct preliminary market scanning to get the prevailing market price or make use of the existing price data of the DOH or other relevant agencies to ensure that the government get the most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The BAC had always looked for deals and transactions that are most advantageous to the government. They undertook steps, such as, but not limited to, market scanning and referral to existing price data to ensure that the government will get the best and/or most advantageous price of the units. The Request for Quotation was served to three interested bidders with a unit price based on the price offered by the Lowest Calculated Responsive Bid. The Provincial Health Officer, as BAC Member, informed the BAC Chairperson that the price of the scanner was taken from the DOH Memorandum Circulars No. 2020-0131 dated March 23, 2020, and No. 2020-0186 dated April 5, 2020, or the List for the Price Freeze of Essential Emergency Medicines and Medical Devices due to the COVID-19 (COVID-19) were issued after the Provincial Government purchased the items in February 2020. Therefore, it was clear that the end-user, represented by the Provincial Health Officer, conducted price inquiry and research only after the procurement process had been completed. Given the foregoing, Management believed that the BAC could not be faulted for “overpricing,” as they chose the cheapest price in the market. Based on the price inquiry and research they conducted, the price of heavy-duty thermal scanner/imager ranged from ₱45,000.00 to ₱65,000.00 per unit.
observations.reason: The complete detailed specifications of the thermal scanners were not indicated in the PO and the AIR but only “thermal handheld scanner (heavy duty)”, thus, it was difficult to determine the fairness of the ABC and the reasonableness of the bid price.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Accountant submit documents to support payments of cash assistance to stranded individuals and vulnerable sectors affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, such as the SP resolution establishing the guidelines and rates of financial assistance, authorization letters for claimants receiving payments in behalf of stranded individuals, proof of receipt by the latter, and list of persons qualified to receive assistance, duly certified by the Mayors or Punong Barangays of the respective local government units where they came from.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The PSWDO had submitted a partial list duly signed by the cash assistance recipients.
observations.reason: There was a lack of personnel to collect/consolidate the list of recipients in the different barangays of the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the PEMO to monitor the submission of the approved SWMPs of the Municipalities of San Juan, Maria and Lazi, and thereafter, enjoin the PSWMB to fast track the formulation of the 10-year SWMP of the Province.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The Provincial Government, through the PPDO, had followed up on the municipalities that could not submit their respective Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: The Municipalities of Maria and Lazi had not yet submitted the said plan. In CY 2019, said LGUs submitted their SWMPs to the EMB in Region VII for review but had not been returned yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct all municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit the required data for consolidation in the provincial level, and to direct the provincial GFPS to establish and maintain a GAD Database containing an improved sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics as bases in the formulation and inclusion of gender responsive issues in the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 The PPDO consolidated the data submitted and incorporated into the Provincial GAD Database. The office had initiated data gathering to offices and stakeholders of the four municipalities that could not submit their GAD-related data.
observations.reason: Four municipalities were not able to submit their GAD-related data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; (b) direct the Provincial Accountant to monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled or properly accounted for; and (c) instruct the Provincial Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the Circular and, if warranted, request for write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 Reminder/Demand Letters were sent to the concerned accountable officers for their unliquidated cash advances. No additional cash advance was granted unless the previous ones were liquidated. The unliquidated cash advances were deducted from the subsequent cash advance drawn.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers were already separated from service due to retirement, death, and unknown whereabouts. As of December 31, 2021, the Statement of Financial Position showed a balance of ₱7,443,486.25 under the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer. The amount includes cash advances for the Confidential Fund of ₱4,020,000.00, the related documents to liquidate were already submitted to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Office (ICFAO) of the Commission on Audit on various dates for post-audit. However, the agency did not receive Transmittal Letter duly received by ICFAO as the basis for recording liquidation in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management issue a Warning Letter to defaulting Contractor and require the submission of a Catch-up Plan to complete the works within the approved contract period. Henceforth, the Provincial Engineer shall continue to monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that these are completed within the target dates, ensuring that the needed infrastructure facilities are provided to the constituents at the earliest possible time.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 The project engineers conducted constant monitoring of the projects to ensure their implementation. Liquidated damages were imposed on delayed completion.
observations.reason: Several projects were suspended due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management shall: a. Provide an appropriation for the LGU of Lazi to collect, construction of garbage storage area with proper division for segregation of wastes; b. Install a security fence on the storage area with properly marked warning sign as provided in Health Care Waste Management Manual; c. Coordinate with other government agencies for the possible collection, transport and disposal of accumulated hospital wastes; and d. Regularly inspect and maintain the cleanliness of the storage area as well as the surroundings of the hospital in compliance with the provisions of the Health Care Waste Management Manual.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 Infectious, hazardous and sharp wastes were placed in a newly constructed placenta pit at the Lazi Medicare Community Hospital.
observations.reason: There was an absence of an accredited forwarder to collect and transport pathological wastes in the Province. A sanitary landfill was not available in the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Treasurer, in coordination with Provincial Cooperative Development Officer, to exert and intensify the collection of outstanding loans by sending final demand letter. Further, it is recommended that Management take legal action for those coop borrowers who are still operational and have capacity to pay but continuously refuse to make repayment of their outstanding loans.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 Demand letters were already sent to the concerned cooperatives and associations. A dialogue with the active cooperatives/associations was also conducted. Lastly, the Management attended said cooperatives’ Board of Director’s Meeting to inform them of their outstanding accounts with the Provincial Government.
observations.reason: Some cooperatives/associations with outstanding loans no longer exist and have ceased operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the settlement of the audit disallowance in accordance with the payment schedule granted by COA Legal Services Sector, Prosecution and Litigation Office.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 Refunds of the disallowed amount were deducted from the payroll of employees still in service, and terminal pay of those who have retired.
observations.reason: The subject disallowance was not fully settled. Some of the persons liable were already separated from the service, while some are deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 by requiring the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents for timely auditorial review and technical evaluation.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 Perfected contracts of the infrastructure projects and Purchase Orders of goods and services were submitted to the Auditor.
observations.reason: The perfected contract and the POs were still submitted after the reglementary period due to limited manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the GAD Focal Person to initiate the formulation and development of a local GAD Code based on the gender issues and concerns identified through consultation with women constituents of the Province, coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and make representation with the local Sangguniang Panlalawigan for eventual passage and approval thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The GAD Focal Person had coordinated with the DILG for orientation in the formulation of GAD Code. The gathering had asked for assistance of needed data for GAD Code from DILG in formulating the GAD Code.
observations.reason: There was insufficient data on gender issues within the Province as the basis in the formulation of the Local GAD Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the department heads to submit their usage of the various supplies and materials, thru the RIS, to the Property Custodian so that the latter can prepare the required SSMI which will be the basis for recording the expenses in the books of account.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 Adjustments had already been made in the books.
observations.reason: There were no documents to support the issuance of gasoline, oil and lubricants purchased in previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revise the contract of service to include the specific job description to support the hiring of contractual and job orders. Direct the immediate supervisors to review carefully the accomplishment reports to ensure that specific and preferably quantifiable activities actually performed are stated to justify the amount of wages paid to hired workers.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 A certification from the agency head that the services of the job orders were necessary and enumerating the works to be done had been attached to the Contract of Service.
observations.reason: Some accountable departments did not quantify the targets and actual accomplishments in the job order’s accomplishment reports. The accomplishments were expressed in terms of percentage instead of quantity and the basis for the computation of the percentage of accomplishment was not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Provincial Accountant to comply strictly with the provision of Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995, on the timely submission of the accounts. Otherwise, impose administrative disciplinary action under Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 The Provincial Accountant had submitted the accounts and financial reports.
observations.reason: There was still a delay in the submission of the monthly Trial Balance and accounts of the Province. Quarterly financial statements were not prepared due to lack of personnel and the voluminous workload in the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Provincial Accountant to book up the value of the equipment received from the Regional Health Office and the General Services Officer to prepare a list of unserviceable PPE to be taken out from the books.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 The equipment from the Regional Health Office was already recorded in the books.
observations.reason: The documents pertaining to the donated equipment from a German organization were not yet available as basis for recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the responsible official to prepare and submit the SSMI to the accountant as basis in recording issuances in the books.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 The SSMI had already been prepared for the current purchases. Issuances of prior years’ inventory were not supported with Requisition Issue Slips and no summary of issuances was prepared and submitted to the Accounting Office as basis in recording. Some of these inventories have already been utilized/issued but the documents were not available as basis for the adjustment in the books. No physical count was conducted on inventories in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Some of these inventories have already been utilized/issued but the documents were not available as basis for the adjustment in the books. No physical count was conducted on inventories in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of PEI and henceforth to observe strictly the limitations on the grant of PEI.
observations.managementAction: CY 2010 Refunds were made through payroll deduction of staff in service, while the full amount of disallowance was deducted from claims for terminal pay of the accountable officer.
observations.reason: The Province had difficulty collecting the refunds of the retired payees, and other payees were deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO (Supply Officer) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property records and reconcile the same.
observations.managementAction: The balances of the inventory accounts had decreased in the succeeding quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation for the apparent disregard of the basic requirements on pakyaw labor contracting, particularly on clearly defined schedule of work, procurement of labor component of the project only, provision of tools to the pakyaw group, and notarized pakyaw agreement. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: Directed the GAD Focal Person to initiate activities for the establishment of a local GAD code based on the gender issues and concerns in their respective localities identified through consultation with women constituents. Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) in the formulation and development of the said Code. Make representation with the local law-making body for the eventual passage and approval of the Local GAD code.
observations.managementAction: The local legislative had not formulated the GAD Code yet.

observations.recommendation: Consider terminating the time deposits in the Trust Funds and ensure that only idle funds in the General Fund are used in the opening of time deposit accounts pursuant to Section 21 of COA Circulars No. 92-382 and to ensure that funds are readily available, when needed.
observations.managementAction: Part of the fund was transferred to GF to address the emergency brought about by the pandemic.
observations.reason: Current balance of the DRRMF placed under time deposit amounted to P7,354,571.19.

observations.recommendation: Implement all measures to arrive at a favorable consensus with resistant beneficiaries. Initiate rerouting of pipeline to ensure delivery of services to interested beneficiaries. Proper consultation should be conducted in the identification of project beneficiaries in the future.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to reroute pipelines and no new source was drilled.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of the long overdue accounts from local and national agencies and private individuals. If same could not be collected, request for write-off of these accounts at the COA Central Office.
observations.managementAction: No request for write-off was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
reconcile their records and coordinate with the Registry of Deeds for the titled lots
in the name of the Municipality. We further recommended that registration and
transfer of title of the land owned by the Municipality be prioritized to fully protect
its interest.

We recommended that Management require a) the Inventory Committee to
properly fill up the RPCPPE; b) the Municipal Treasurer to have the Acknowledgment
Receipts for Equipment/Property Acknowledgement Receipts renewed/updated; and
c) the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the
discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer (MDRRMO) coordinate with the Punong Barangays for the
submission of beneficiary/household profiles and require written authorization from
recipients other than the identified target beneficiaries. We further recommended
that the MDRRMO submit an explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop recording purchases
of non-expendable supplies directly to the related expense account and record the
purchases and utilization of these items in accordance with Sections 113 and 120
of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, in order to fairly present the asset
and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a special account in
the General Fund to record the receipt and utilization of the Bayanihan grant to the
Municipality pursuant to LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 to facilitate monitoring
of the receipt and utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management utilize the LDRRMF in accordance with
Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March
25, 2013. We further recommended that Management stop charging
honorarium/incentive against the LDRMMF.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of procurement
through reimbursement and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 and relevant GPPB issuances to ensure transparency, economy
and efficiency in the procurement process. We also recommended that Management
comply with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the
grant and utilization of cash advances. We further recommended that the
Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before
processing any claims.

We recommended that Management stop transferring unexpended
LDRRMF from the STF to GF before the lapse of five years and to require the
Municipal Accountant to record charges in the STF in accordance with COA
Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management prepare and furnish the Auditor a copy
of the (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt
by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining
undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-
009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the
end-user duly authorized by the Head of the PE to properly and completely fill up
the relevant and required information in the POs to allow comparison with the
prevailing market prices in the locality, existing price data of the agency, the
Department of Trade and Industry or other relevant agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of
transferring savings from the completed projects under the 20% DF to the Trust
Fund for the payment of honorarium to the municipal officials and employees
involved in government procurement.
We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should
see to it that sources of funds for the payment of honoraria are in accordance with
the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007. In
case of deficiency in the collections from procurement activities and non-availability
of savings, the amount of honoraria shall be adjusted proportionately for all those
entitled thereto in accordance with Section 3.5 of the Budget Circular.

We recommend and Management agreed to submit justification/s for the
noted deficiencies. We further recommend that, henceforth, Management adhere to
the provisions of DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2 dated September 23,
2016 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 97-273 dated October 30, 1997 in the
conduct of lakbay-aral or benchmarking activity.

We recommended that Management refrain from holding seminars/
workshops outside of the province in accordance with pertinent sections of COA
Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012to avoid extravagant expenditures and
wastage of government funds.

We recommended that Management ensure compliance with Section
62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the required warranty security,
otherwise, they will be held answerable for negligence in case of structural defects
or failures within the warranty period not covered by the surety bond.

We recommend and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare one payroll
for each office for the monthly salaries of officials and employees, with all the
allowable deductions, and prepare two separate cash advance vouchers equivalent
to the net amount of the payroll covering the pay period for the 15th and end of the
month.

We recommended that the Assistant Municipal Treasurer - ICO coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and verify the existence of over deposit and for the latter to make the appropriate adjustment, if warranted. We further recommended that she, henceforth, deposit her collections intact daily or on the next banking day and set a cut-off time for collections during Fridays to ensure that these are deposited on the same day.
observations.managementAction: A Journal Entry Voucher was taken up in the books for the over deposit.
observations.reason: Collections were deposited intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) the Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Accountant review and reconcile the discrepancy of P827,296.20 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of the RPCPPE and ledger cards; (b) the Municipal Agriculturist provide the Municipal Accountant a report of livestock as basis for recording any additions to as well as deductions from the Biological Assets account; and (c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain WOABSLCs as required under Section 114 of the Manual.
observations.managementAction: The inventory report reconciled with accounting records
observations.reason: The inventory report was not completely filled up while AREs were not updated ( Reiterated in Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for expenses other than those items covered under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: No more charges for meals and snacks in the LDRRM funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exercise due prudence in the utilization of the scarce resources of the government. Limit the incurrence of expenses for meals, snacks and other foodstuff during events to the amount of appropriations for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses and stop the practice of holding receptions or serving meals and snacks during celebrations and gatherings and the charging thereof to government funds except to the representation allowance of officials concerned. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant record the expenses in their proper accounts.
observations.managementAction: Expenditures of the same nature were reduced to the minimum.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Acting Municipal Treasurer issue OR for inter-fund transfers made in accordance with Section 68 (1) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Inter-fund transfers were fully receipted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer remit to the taxes withheld on compensation as well as those on purchases of goods and services within ten (10) days after at the end of each month as prescribed under the BIR regulation.
observations.managementAction: Taxes withheld from salaries/wages and from suppliers were remitted timely.

observations.recommendation: Required the Local School Board to explain and submit the legal basis of including the expense items under the SEF budget. Henceforth, ensure that only those priority expense items as enumerated under Section 4.0 of DEPED/DBM/DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2017 are included in the SEF budget approved and enacted.
observations.managementAction: Questioned expenditures no longer included in the current budget.

observations.recommendation: Consider to transfer the deposits maintained with First Consolidated Bank to authorized government depository banks, to eliminate exposure of government funds from certain risks particularly on insolvency and bank closure.
observations.managementAction: Accounts maintained FCB were transferred to DBP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LGU officials to submit within the reglementary period approved copy of the GAD plan and Budget and ensure that the annual accomplishment report were submitted on time.
observations.managementAction: The separate GAD Plan and Budget was presented in the annual budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all movable properties of the Municipality; (b) the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO to prepare the report on the results of the count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results with the accounting records; and (c) the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO as property custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and ledgers cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The inventory report reconciled with accounting records
observations.reason: The inventory report was not completely filled up while AREs were not updated ( Reiterated in Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the documentary requirements, such as the (a) number of personnel involved and their corresponding rates/salary; (b) schedule of work and places of assignments or station; (c) group classification of personnel to determine the Equivalent Equipment Monthly Statutory Minimum Wage Rate in accordance with the applicable Rule Implementing RA No. 6727; and (c) approved documents indicating the minimum requirements of the agency on the number of security personnel to be involved in the project. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Assistant Municipal Treasurer - ICO coordinate
with the Municipal Accountant and verify the existence of over deposit and for the
latter to make the appropriate adjustment, if warranted. We further recommended
that she, henceforth, deposit her collections intact daily or on the next banking day
and set a cut-off time for collections during Fridays to ensure that these are
deposited on the same day.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee
to conduct a physical count of inventories and prepare the Report of the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO (Supply
Officer) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property records and reconcile
the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct (a) the Inventory
Committee to conduct a physical count of all movable properties of the
Municipality; (b) the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO to prepare the report on
the results of the count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results with the
accounting records; and (c) the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO as property
custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and ledgers
cards, respectively.

We recommend that (a) the Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Accountant
review and reconcile the discrepancy of P827,296.20 and henceforth, conduct
periodic reconciliation of the RPCPPE and ledger cards; (b) the Municipal
Agriculturist provide the Municipal Accountant a report of livestock as basis for
recording any additions to as well as deductions from the Biological Assets account;
and (c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain WOABSLCs as required under
Section 114 of the Manual.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the
documentary requirements, such as the (a) number of personnel involved and their
corresponding rates/salary; (b) schedule of work and places of assignments or
station; (c) group classification of personnel to determine the Equivalent Equipment
Monthly Statutory Minimum Wage Rate in accordance with the applicable Rule
Implementing RA No. 6727; and (c) approved documents indicating the minimum
requirements of the agency on the number of security personnel to be involved in
the project.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant review
and ensure the completeness of documentation in the processing of payments for
services in accordance with Item 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June
14, 2012.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit an
explanation for the apparent disregard of the basic requirements on pakyaw labor
contracting, particularly on clearly defined schedule of work, procurement of labor
component of the project only, provision of tools to the pakyaw group, and
notarized pakyaw agreement. We further recommended that, henceforth, the
Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported
with complete documentation to establish the validity and propriety of the
transactions.

We recommended that the Municipality stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for
expenses other than those items covered under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management exercise due prudence in the utilization
of the scarce resources of the government. Limit the incurrence of expenses for
meals, snacks and other foodstuff during events to the amount of appropriations for
Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses and stop the practice of holding
receptions or serving meals and snacks during celebrations and gatherings and the
charging thereof to government funds except to the representation allowance of
officials concerned. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant
record the expenses in their proper accounts.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Acting Municipal Treasurer issue
OR for inter-fund transfers made in accordance with Section 68 (1) of P.D. No.
1445.

Any claims for repair of structures within the warranty period will be disallowed in audit.
