Circular No. 2014-1.

Require MF Construction to remove and replace the Portland Cement Concrete Pavement which was severely affected by major scaling and to further require the contractor to make appropriate corrections on the other deficiencies noted, in the equivalent amount of P1,240,002.72.
observations.managementAction: Management required the contractor to correct the deficiencies noted in the inspection of project.
observations.reason: Ocular inspection conducted by the Audit Team showed that repairs/corrections were already done by the contractor.

observations.recommendation: Require concerned agency official/ accountable officer to immediately liquidate the cash advance as soon as the purpose for which it was granted has already been completed.
observations.managementAction: Release of special cash advances were already stopped.
observations.reason: Post-audit of liquidation reports showed that the release of a single cash advance for the whole activity was stopped, however special cash advances per specific activity was released to different bonded officials/employee of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: Authorize the grant of special cash advance only for the payment of current operating expenses where it is impractical to pay the same by check in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Checks were drawn in the name of the payees.
observations.reason: Validation conducted by the Audit Team showed that checks were issued to those expenses which could be readily paid by checks.

observations.recommendation: Instruct concerned agency official/accountable officer ensure that individual expenditures charged to the cash advance are supported with the proper documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were fully submitted
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursements showed that these nature were now supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of utilizing funds for entertainment and other unnecessary expenses to avoid wastage of the scarce resources of the government.
observations.managementAction: Expenses for fireworks display was stopped and payment of the disallowance to be initiated.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, post-audit of transaction documents showed that the municipality has not disbursed any amount for fireworks display.

observations.recommendation: Submit the legal basis in charging expenses totaling P175,298.59 against the SEF. Henceforth, stop the practice of providing appropriation for regular MOOE of the schools and to strictly observe Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG JC No.1 as amended on the SEF utilization.
observations.managementAction: The said disbursement to SEF has long been stopped.
observations.reason: Post-audit of charges to SEF showed no disbursements for expenses chargeable to the regular MOOE.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the GAD Focal Person to exert extra effort in implementing identified activities in accordance with the GAD Plan in order to fully utilize available funds in accordance programs of the DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: The said projects were already implemented.
observations.reason: Verification conducted showed that these GAD-related programs/projects were already implemented.

observations.recommendation: Consider to transfer the deposits maintained with First Consolidated Bank to authorized government depository banks, to eliminate exposure of government funds from certain risks particularly on insolvency and bank closure.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that it has already transferred the current accounts to the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP).
observations.reason: Bank Statements attached to the Bank Reconciliation Statements confirmed the transfer of the current accounts of the Municipality to the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP). However time deposits maintained at FCB were not yet transferred.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer Designee to issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to concerned municipal officials and employees to pinpoint responsibility and accountability for municipal properties.
observations.managementAction: The issuance of PAR/ARE is on-going.
observations.reason: These PARs/AREs were not yet submitted to the Auditor for validation.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from paying transactions thru reimbursement basis pursuant to Section 93 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, in order not to circumvent the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursements were limited to small items only.
observations.reason: Post-audit of supplies reimbursements showed that reimbursements were limited to small items only since the Municipality does not maintain a Petty Cash Fund.

observations.recommendation: Direct all officials and employees with outstanding cash advance to immediately liquidate the same. Stop granting additional cash advance if the previous ones have not been settled/liquidated yet pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445. Exert strong efforts to collect or cause the liquidation of cash advances made by employees not anymore in service. Coordinate from other government agencies in case of transfer by former employees. Henceforth, observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and prescribed Travel Law to preclude disallowances in audit. Refrain from clearing any official or employee from money accountability if he/she still has an unliquidated cash advance with the government.
observations.managementAction: Management has initiated monitoring/control on liquidation/releases of cash advances.
observations.reason: Verification of cash advance report at year-end showed that except for the Confidential and Intelligence Fund which has long been outstanding, the rest of the cash advances were considered current.

observations.recommendation: Require the personnel in charge in the management of fund to intensify efforts to locate the beneficiary/ borrower. Send demand letters in a periodic basis before employing legal means to collect the receivables. Conduct proper screening of would be beneficiaries. Conduct periodic monitoring of the borrower’s performance and steps to assist the beneficiaries in problems encountered has to be handy and available. Make available a continuing training on capability building and technical assistance for the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal have started sending collection letters and encourage the beneficiaries to pay even on minimal amounts so as to reduce the outstanding balance.
observations.reason: As noted during the verification of collections, there were minimal repayments from the beneficiaries of the said projects.

observations.recommendation: Require the Immediate refund of the overpayment as shown on the attached Notice of Disallowance No. 2011-001-100(10) dated March 21, 2011 is hereby recommended. Henceforth, stop the practice of paying benefits more than what is authorized by laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The disallowance was already recorded in the books and repayment of such was initiated.
observations.reason: Verification in the books of accounts of the Municipality showed that the Notice of Disallowance was already recorded and refund thereof was initiated.

observations.recommendation: Direct all persons held liable therefore to immediately settle in full the disallowed amount so received otherwise, legal measures shall be resorted to if only to collect the same.
observations.managementAction: Repayments were made monthly through payroll deductions.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll confirmed the deduction of the refund of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: Direct each recipient of the excessive amount of PEI to refund the sum not legally due them or the aggregate amount of P1,495,000.00. Henceforth, observe strictly the guidelines prescribed in the grant of allowances and other benefits to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The disallowance was already recorded in the books and repayment of such was initiated.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll confirmed the deduction of the refund of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the properties in the name of the municipality by coordinating with the Municipal Assessor, DENR and other related agencies for the processing of the needed documents.
observations.managementAction: Management is waiting for Free titling from the DENR The free titling from the DENR is not for all types of lot. No budget was set aside by the Municipality to secure the documents needed if indeed some of these lots would pass the free titling assessment of the DENR.
